name: input-group
description: A fused-border group wrapper that composes one CORE form control (input, native-select, or any element) with optional leading/trailing addons and peer elements into one visually cohesive unit. The group is purely presentational: layout shell + shared border + focus-within ring; all state lives in the inner controls. Slots: content (required, the core control), leadingElement / trailingElement (full-peer controls with shared border), leadingAddon / trailingAddon (decorative or lightweight-interactive pieces on muted background). size is height-based (sm/md/lg) so the group aligns flush with buttons and inputs in a toolbar row. role="group" is emitted only when ariaLabel or ariaLabelledBy is set (WAI-ARIA grouping rule). Invalid state: aria-invalid + destructive ring. No enhancer, no client state.
docs: Copies the input-group partial to your JTE root at lievit/input-group.jte, reached as @template.lievit.input-group(...). The `content` slot (required) holds the core form control; `leadingAddon` / `trailingAddon` take decorative pieces (icons, text labels) with a muted background; `leadingElement` / `trailingElement` take full-peer controls (selects, buttons) that share the group border. size (sm/md/lg) is height-based so the group lines up with form controls in a toolbar row. Set ariaLabel or ariaLabelledBy when the group has a shared semantic label (triggers role="group"); omit for single-field usage where the inner input has its own label. Set invalid=true for the destructive border + ring treatment.
